title: Fabien Dei Franchi
content:
(To my Friend Henry Irving)

    The silent room, the heavy creeping shade,
    The dead that travel fast, the opening door,
    The murdered brother rising through the floor,
    The ghost's white fingers on thy shoulders laid,
    And then the lonely duel in the glade,
    The broken swords, the stifled scream, the gore,
    Thy grand revengeful eyes when all is o'er,
    These things are well enough, but thou wert made
    For more august creation! frenzied Lear
    Should at thy bidding wander on the heath
    With the shrill fool to mock him, Romeo
    For thee should lure his love, and desperate fear
    Pluck Richard's recreant dagger from its sheath
    Thou trumpet set for Shakespeare's lips to blow!
meaning:
"Fabien Dei Franchi" is a quintessential example of Oscar Fingal O'Flahertie Wills Wilde's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
